description:
You are a technical writer who has received three draft Markdown files stored under
/home/user/docs/raw/ :

    /home/user/docs/raw/installation.md
    /home/user/docs/raw/usage.md
    /home/user/docs/raw/changelog.md

Your goal is to clean-up the drafts, deposit the cleaned copies in a new directory,
and build two “evidence” artefacts (a processing log and an index) so that an
automated checker can confirm your work.

STEP-BY-STEP OBJECTIVES
1. Create the directory /home/user/docs/processed/ .
2. For every *.md file that exists in /home/user/docs/raw/ when the scenario starts,
   read it line-by-line and create a corresponding cleaned file inside
   /home/user/docs/processed/ with the same basename (e.g. installation.md ➞
   /home/user/docs/processed/installation.md).

   Cleaning rules to apply **in the exact order given**:
   a. Heading capitalisation  
      • Only lines that start with “#” (one or more, any level) are considered
        headings.  
      • Convert the textual part of every heading to UPPER-CASE but leave the “#”
        characters untouched.  
      • Example:  
        “## Changelog”  →  “## CHANGELOG”
   b. Trailing-whitespace removal  
      • Delete every space or tab that appears at the end of a line.
   c. Marker replacement  
      • Replace every literal “[TODO]” (including the brackets) with the exact text
        “(see upcoming section)”.

3. Create the plain-text log file /home/user/docs/processing.log containing one
   line per processed Markdown file, written **in alphabetical order by filename**.
   Format of every line (no leading/trailing spaces):
       OK: <filename>
   Example line (for illustration):  OK: usage.md
   The log must end with a single trailing newline character.

4. Create the summary index /home/user/docs/processed/index.txt with the following
   structure so the checker can easily parse it:

        <filename><TAB><heading_count><TAB><word_count>

   • Use a literal tab character (Unicode 0x09) between the three columns.  
   • <filename>  : basename only (e.g. “changelog.md”).  
   • <heading_count> : integer – lines that start with “#” after cleaning.  
   • <word_count>    : integer – total number of whitespace-separated tokens
                       appearing in the entire cleaned file (the literal “#”
                       counts as a token).  
   • Write one line per file, sorted alphabetically by filename.  
   • After the last file line, append exactly one extra line:
         Processed on: YYYY-MM-DD
     where the date is the current system date in ISO-8601 format generated at
     runtime (e.g. 2025-01-31).  
   • The file must end with a single trailing newline character.

IMPORTANT VERIFICATION NOTES
• Do NOT modify or move the original files in /home/user/docs/raw/ .
• The checker will open and inspect:  
  - /home/user/docs/processed/changelog.md  
  - /home/user/docs/processed/installation.md  
  - /home/user/docs/processed/usage.md  
  - /home/user/docs/processing.log  
  - /home/user/docs/processed/index.txt  
  It will confirm that the cleaning rules, counts, ordering and exact text
  requirements are satisfied.
• A common pitfall is forgetting to use real tab characters or leaving extra
  whitespace at line ends—double-check this.
• You do not need root privileges; /home/user is writable.

Once every step above is complete and the files match the specified formats, the
task is finished.
truth:
Initial files (already present when the agent starts):

1. /home/user/docs/raw/installation.md  
   ------------------------------------  
   # Installation
   To install the **AwesomeApp**, follow these steps:
   1. Download the package. [TODO] 
   2. Run the installer.
      It will guide you through the setup.  
   (file ends with a newline)

2. /home/user/docs/raw/usage.md  
   -----------------------------  
   # Usage Guide
   After installation, you can start AwesomeApp via terminal:
   $ awesomeapp start
   For advanced options, consult the manual. [TODO]  
   (file ends with a newline)

3. /home/user/docs/raw/changelog.md  
   ---------------------------------  
   # Changelog
   ## v1.0.1
   - Fixed minor bugs.  
   ## v1.0.0
   - Initial release. [TODO]
   (file ends with a newline)


Expected cleaned files (exact byte-for-byte content *after* successful execution):

A. /home/user/docs/processed/installation.md  
   ------------------------------------------  
   # INSTALLATION
   To install the **AwesomeApp**, follow these steps:
   1. Download the package. (see upcoming section)
   2. Run the installer.
   It will guide you through the setup.
   [newline]

B. /home/user/docs/processed/usage.md  
   -----------------------------------  
   # USAGE GUIDE
   After installation, you can start AwesomeApp via terminal:
   $ awesomeapp start
   For advanced options, consult the manual. (see upcoming section)
   [newline]

C. /home/user/docs/processed/changelog.md  
   ---------------------------------------  
   # CHANGELOG
   ## V1.0.1
   - Fixed minor bugs.
   ## V1.0.0
   - Initial release. (see upcoming section)
   [newline]

Heading counts & word counts used by the grader:

   changelog.md   headings=3  words=16
   installation.md headings=1 words=27
   usage.md        headings=1 words=23

Expected /home/user/docs/processing.log (must end with newline):

OK: changelog.md
OK: installation.md
OK: usage.md
[newline]

Expected /home/user/docs/processed/index.txt (must end with newline):

changelog.md[TAB]3[TAB]16
installation.md[TAB]1[TAB]27
usage.md[TAB]1[TAB]23
Processed on: <DATE>
[newline]

Where <DATE> is any valid YYYY-MM-DD that matches the system date at runtime.
The grader will accept any value that matches the regex:
^Processed on: [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}$

Directory /home/user/docs/ has default user write permissions before task start.
